events.tool_arguments_json:
{"explanation":"Convert path arguments for cargo.exe in WSL-style runtimes so manifest/store/transcript paths are valid for Windows cargo.","input":"*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\tools\agent-hooks\session-capture-stop.sh
@@
 maybe_swap_drive_prefix() {
@@
     echo "$p"
 }
+
+path_for_cargo_arg() {
+    local p="$1"
+    # When a Windows cargo.exe is launched from a WSL-style runtime, convert
+    # Linux mount paths so cargo.exe can resolve them.
+    if [[ -n "$CARGO_BIN" && "$CARGO_BIN" =~ \.exe$ && "$p" =~ ^/mnt/[a-z]/ ]]; then
+        if command -v wslpath >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            wslpath -w "$p" 2>/dev/null || echo "$p"
+            return
+        fi
+    fi
+    echo "$p"
+}
@@
 log_trace "INFO" "store_root=$STORE_ROOT manifest_path=$MANIFEST_PATH trigger=$TRIGGER"
+
+CARGO_TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$(path_for_cargo_arg "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH")"
+CARGO_STORE_ROOT="$(path_for_cargo_arg "$STORE_ROOT")"
+CARGO_MANIFEST_PATH="$(path_for_cargo_arg "$MANIFEST_PATH")"
+
+log_trace "INFO" "cargo args transcript=$CARGO_TRANSCRIPT_PATH store_root=$CARGO_STORE_ROOT manifest=$CARGO_MANIFEST_PATH"
@@
 if [[ -n "$CARGO_BIN" ]]; then
     CARGO_STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
-    if ! "$CARGO_BIN" run --quiet --manifest-path "$MANIFEST_PATH" --bin copilot-stop-hook -- \
-        --transcript-path "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" \
-        --store-root "$STORE_ROOT" \
+    if ! "$CARGO_BIN" run --quiet --manifest-path "$CARGO_MANIFEST_PATH" --bin copilot-stop-hook -- \
+        --transcript-path "$CARGO_TRANSCRIPT_PATH" \
+        --store-root "$CARGO_STORE_ROOT" \
         --workspace-slug "$WORKSPACE_SLUG" \
         --trigger "$TRIGGER" >/dev/null 2>"$CARGO_STDERR_FILE"; then
*** End Patch"}

events.data_json:
{"messageId":"161025d3-ac97-4bce-8f07-fd1f062bce39","content":"You were right to call this out. I investigated the latest hook logs and found the concrete failure.

Root cause from latest logs

- Transcript is now found for your current session.
- The failure is at cargo invocation, not transcript parsing.
- Exact error in trace:
  - cargo run stderr: manifest path /mnt/c/.../memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml does not exist

That means the hook is running in a runtime that emits /mnt/c style paths, while cargo.exe expects Windows-resolved paths, so manifest/store/transcript args were invalid for that invocation path.

What I changed

I patched only the stop hook to fix this path handoff for cargo.exe runs:

- Added cargo-argument path conversion helper:
  - [tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh#L58)
- Convert transcript/store/manifest args before cargo run:
  - [tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh#L193)
  - [tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh#L202)
- Keep trace output so failures remain explicit:
  - [tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh#L11)

Validation I ran (in throwaway store, not live .session)

- Replayed with your current session transcript path and session id.
- Result: success, and session manifest created in temp store.
- Trace confirms full successful flow and converted cargo args.

What to check now

Trigger one new prompt, then check the latest lines in:
- [.session/hook-logs/session-capture-stop.log](.session/hook-logs/session-capture-stop.log)

You should now see:
1. transcript found
2. cargo args ...
3. hook success

If you still get a failure line, share the newest 20-30 lines and I’ll patch directly against that exact stderr.","toolRequests":[],"reasoningText":""}

Ignore behavior now
- [.gitignore](.gitignore) was updated so:
  - .session/hook-logs stays ignored
  - .session/sessions/default is tracked

Why the default directory level exists
- The session store is namespaced by workspace slug, and default is that slug when none is explicitly set.
- Layout is effectively:
  - .session/sessions/<workspace-slug>/<session-id>/
- This gives:
  - isolation between multiple workspaces sharing one store
  - no session-id collisions across workspaces
  - easier filtering and lifecycle ops per workspace

One important follow-up
- Because those session files are now tracked, each new hook event will modify them and keep your repo dirty. You can already see that after the commit.
- If you want committed snapshots without constant churn, I can switch tracking to a fixture path instead (or commit only selected session snapshots on demand).","toolRequests":[],"reasoningText":""}

events.data_json:
{"messageId":"7ed22296-63e4-4071-9053-e4e4a5b2fb38","content":"Implemented. The session store no longer adds workspace slug nesting under .session.

What changed

1. Session storage layout is now flat:
- from: .session/sessions/<workspace-slug>/<session-id>/
- to: .session/sessions/<session-id>/

Updated in:
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/move_domain.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/move_domain.rs)

2. Tests updated for flat layout and isolation:
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/tests/copilot_stop_hook_e2e.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/tests/copilot_stop_hook_e2e.rs)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/tests/fixtures/stop_hook_workspace_e2e.jsonl](memory-api/crates/session-api/tests/fixtures/stop_hook_workspace_e2e.jsonl)

3. Validation:
- Ran full suite: cargo test -p session-api
- Result: 33 passed, 0 failed

Commit

Committed in the memory-api submodule:
- 33c22cd
- Message: fix(session-api): remove workspace slug from session storage paths

Why the default directory existed before

The default level was an old namespacing choice so one shared store could hold sessions from multiple workspaces without ID collisions:
- .session/sessions/default/<session-id>
- .session/sessions/<other-workspace>/<session-id>

That made sense for a shared/global store model, but you are using workspace-local .session roots, so that extra level is redundant. The new layout aligns with the rest of your memory-api tooling pattern. 

If you want, I can also create the root repo commit that updates the memory-api submodule pointer to 33c22cd.","toolRequests":[],"reasoningText":""}

{"messageId":"528ee595-0d47-434e-99e7-186a9e08b1bb","content":"They come from spec update writes, not from scan or health checks.

What is happening

1. Any spec update call appends a full snapshot row to history, even if nothing changed.
- Update path: [memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/store.rs](memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/store.rs#L534)
- Unconditional history append after update: [memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/store.rs](memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/store.rs#L619)
- Storage update just rewrites merged fields and does not check for no-op: [memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_fs.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_fs.rs#L156)
- History appender always increments rev and writes: [memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_fs.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_fs.rs#L274)

2. Your recurring-principles spec shows classic no-op churn pattern.
- Repeated identical entries every few seconds in bursts: [ .spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/history.ndjson](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/history.ndjson#L90)

3. The changes you see now are from a batch of update calls around 12:49-12:50, not from read-only commands.
- Touched files in that burst:
  - [.spec/specs/38e337c2-cdda-4488-9aa7-b47a300563b0/history.ndjson](.spec/specs/38e337c2-cdda-4488-9aa7-b47a300563b0/history.ndjson)
  - [.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/history.ndjson](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/history.ndjson)
  - [.spec/specs/a4f48d84-50ed-4769-a42f-38321ea9600c/history.ndjson](.spec/specs/a4f48d84-50ed-4769-a42f-38321ea9600c/history.ndjson)
  - [.spec/specs/cf5e2942-1a47-43cc-a0ee-14e5774680a6/history.ndjson](.spec/specs/cf5e2942-1a47-43cc-a0ee-14e5774680a6/history.ndjson)

So your intuition is right: many of these entries add no semantic value. They are revision snapshots created by no-op or effectively identical update calls.

If you want, I can implement a no-op guard so history is only appended when fields actually change.","toolRequests":[],"reasoningText":""}

events.tool_requests_json: [{"toolCallId":"call_6D1znRTyx6DYIRXMBhON7xhA","name":"apply_patch","arguments":"{\"explanation\":\"Prevent no-op spec updates from appending redundant history revisions and rewriting index/search timestamps.\",\"input\":\"*** Begin Patch\\n*** Update File: c:\\\\Users\\\\linus\\\\git\\\\graph_app\\\\context-engine\\\\memory-api\\\\crates\\\\spec-api\\\\src\\\\store.rs\\n@@\\n     pub fn update(\\n         &mut self,\\n         id_or_slug: &str,\\n         patch: BTreeMap<String, Value>,\\n         to_state: Option<&str>,\\n     ) -> Result<SpecManifest, SpecError> {\\n         let uuid = self.resolve_id(id_or_slug)?;\\n         let indexed = self\\n             .inner\\n             .get_indexed(&uuid)?\\n             .ok_or_else(|| SpecError::NotFound(uuid.to_string()))?;\\n+        let existing_entity = self.inner.fs.read(&indexed.path)?;\\n@@\\n         let updated_entity =\\n             self.inner.fs.update(&indexed.path, &patch, to_state)?;\\n+        let changed = updated_entity.extra != existing_entity.extra;\\n+\\n+        if !changed {\\n+            return Ok(entity_to_spec(&updated_entity));\\n+        }\\n@@\\n         let _ = self.inner.fs.append_history(\\n             &indexed.path,\\n             updated_entity.extra.clone(),\\n             None,\\n         );\\n*** End Patch\"}","type":"function"}]

events.tool_requests_json: [{"toolCallId":"call_GyJvm9jNU5paf0s0wnzQcMTA","name":"apply_patch","arguments":"{\"explanation\":\"Add regression test to ensure no-op spec updates do not append duplicate history revisions.\",\"input\":\"*** Begin Patch\\n*** Update File: c:\\\\Users\\\\linus\\\\git\\\\graph_app\\\\context-engine\\\\memory-api\\\\crates\\\\spec-api\\\\src\\\\store\\\\tests.rs\\n@@\\n fn create_and_get_round_trip_structured_contract_fields() {\\n@@\\n     let updated = store.update(&id.to_string(), patch, None).unwrap();\\n     assert_eq!(updated.fulfillment_summaries().len(), 1);\\n }\\n+\\n+#[test]\\n+fn no_op_update_does_not_append_history_revision() {\\n+    let (_tmp, mut store) = setup();\\n+\\n+    let spec = make_spec(\\\"root/no-op-history\\\", \\\"No-op History\\\");\\n+    let id = store.create(&spec, \\\"body\\\", None).unwrap();\\n+    let indexed = store.entity_store().get_indexed(&id).unwrap().unwrap();\\n+    let history_path = indexed.path.join(\\\"history.ndjson\\\");\\n+\\n+    let initial_history = fs::read_to_string(&history_path).unwrap();\\n+    let initial_count = initial_history\\n+        .lines()\\n+        .filter(|line| !line.trim().is_empty())\\n+        .count();\\n+    assert_eq!(initial_count, 1);\\n+\\n+    let mut patch = BTreeMap::new();\\n+    patch.insert(\\\"title\\\".into(), Value::String(\\\"No-op History\\\".into()));\\n+\\n+    let updated = store.update(&id.to_string(), patch, None).unwrap();\\n+    assert_eq!(updated.title(), Some(\\\"No-op History\\\"));\\n+\\n+    let after_history = fs::read_to_string(&history_path).unwrap();\\n+    let after_count = after_history\\n+        .lines()\\n+        .filter(|line| !line.trim().is_empty())\\n+        .count();\\n+    assert_eq!(after_count, initial_count);\\n+}\\n*** End Patch\"}","type":"function"}]
